title: Identity vs filing aid: modernizing the catalog without renaming a part
what_changed: The Hardware Store catalog gained a 19-domain engineering taxonomy (catalog/domains.yaml) and a derived addressing + multi-field search tool (parts/store_index.py, the `store` verb). Thirty-one ad-hoc, inconsistently spelled categories were consolidated into stable domains. Zero catalog cards were edited.
why: The catalog organized parts by an ad-hoc `category` string with no stable engineering taxonomy and no addressing scheme, so it read like a language reference, not a parts catalog. V3 asked for a layered identity model (permanent identity, re-classifiable address, display, metadata).
evidence: make check green: 1069 passed, 93.73% coverage; store_index.py 100% covered
The live `store` verb organizes all 40 parts across 19 domains with derived addresses (05.001 circuit-breaker, etc.)
The catalog loader (parts/hardware.py) picks keys explicitly and ignores unknown ones, proven by the existing catalog tests still passing after the change
No card slug or registry designation changed, so the manufacturing loop and completeness gate stayed green
tradeoffs: The catalog address is a derived ordinal that shifts as parts are added (a filing aid, not identity), which is acceptable because the slug id is the stable key. The richer 30-field card template, the seven-stage maturity ladder, and numeric part IDs were deliberately deferred as gated migrations to avoid schema churn now.
future_reuse: store_index's address-derivation-from-taxonomy and multi-field search is itself a reusable catalog pattern (a candidate Hardware Store card). The reserved Caching domain is the next harvest target: the Harvest Lens already flagged a cache pattern recurring across ~8 sites with no card.
concepts: Separate permanent IDENTITY (the slug) from mutable CLASSIFICATION (the address): identity never changes, classification may evolve
'Labels are identity, numbers are filing aids' is an existing CodeForge doctrine the V3 model maps onto exactly
Prefer additive, reversible migrations over renames; a loader that ignores unknown keys is what makes additive schema evolution safe
Frozen identifiers (slugs, verbs, DB columns, seed keys) are a hard constraint that shapes every migration
